title: buildah-containers: add validation to 'format'
body:
Avoid the following situations：

```
➜  buildah git:(containers-format) sudo buildah containers --format zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
zz
```

After change:
```
➜  buildah git:(containers-format) sudo ./buildah containers --format zz
error format: zz
```

Signed-off-by: Zhou Hao <zhouhao@cn.fujitsu.com>

comments.body:
Could you add tests including validating that

--format  "container ID: {{.Id}}"

Works, and add this example to the man page.

>Could you add tests including validating that
--format "container ID: {{.Id}}"

Do you mean this?

sudo ./buildah containers --format "containerID: {{.ContainerID}}"

> i.e. does it hit the error that you have at line 247 or does it do something else?

No. It failed. But it is not because of the change.

```
➜  buildah git:(containers-format) sudo ./buildah containers --format "contaienrID: {{}}"
Template parsing error: template: container:1: missing value for command
```

Thanks for checking on:

```
 buildah git:(containers-format) sudo ./buildah containers --format "contaienrID: {{}}"
Template parsing error: template: container:1: missing value for command
```
That's a valid error in that situation. I was concerned it might go completely off the rails in that instance and not give enough info to the user to figure out what the issue was.

:pushpin: Commit 89d0535 has been approved by `rhatdan`
